final.name: Basalt
final.reasoning: You reported no crystals visible to the naked eye, which rules out the coarse-grained Gabbro, and a clear magnetic response, which fits basalt's typical magnetite content. Combined with the dark, fine groundmass and small gas cavities in the photo, basalt is the best fit.

runner_up.name: Andesite
runner_up.why_still_possible: Andesite is also fine-grained and can look nearly identical in a photo. If the magnetic pull was actually weak and you can spot pale feldspar specks under a lens, andesite moves back into contention.

confirm_with: Scrape the rock hard across an unglazed porcelain tile: basalt and andesite leave little to no streak, so a strong colored streak would tell you it is something else entirely.
caveats: This is a photo plus a few field tests, not a lab analysis. Distinguishing basalt from andesite reliably needs a thin section or chemistry; treat this as 'likely basalt' rather than certain.
